name: Torquemada from Astudillo – circular by Castillo de la Mota and Valdeolmillos
name_original: Torquemada desde Astudillo – circular por Castillo de la Mota y Valdeolmillos
region_intro: Road cycling around Astudillo offers a diverse landscape for cyclists, transitioning from the flat expanses of the "Tierra de Campos" to areas with more rolling hills. The region features a network of lightly trafficked roads, providing varied terrain suitable for different fitness levels. Cyclists can explore routes that traverse open plains or incorporate moderate ascents, often passing through rural charm and historic villages. The Palencia province, where Astudillo is located, is known for its well-maintained cycling infrastructure. Best road cycling routes around Astudillo The most popular road cycling route is Villamediana from Valdeolmillos, a 26.6 miles (42.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 49 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers a balanced ride through the local countryside. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Torquemada from Quintana del Puente – circular by the Castles, a moderate 32.1 miles (51.7 km) path. This route provides scenic views and passes by historical castle sites. Local road cyclists also love the Torquemada from Astudillo – circular by Castillo de la Mota and Valdeolmillos, a 34.0 miles (54.8 km) trail leading through rolling plains and historic villages, often completed in about 2 hours 15 minutes. Road cycling around Astudillo is defined by open plains, rolling hills, and rural landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to more challenging routes with significant elevation gain further north. The routes in Astudillo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.25 stars from over 3 reviews. More than 100 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Astudillo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Astudillo?
A: There are 8 road cycling routes around Astudillo listed on komoot, offering a range of experiences from easy rides to more challenging excursions. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.25 stars.

Q: Are there easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Astudillo?
A: Yes, Astudillo offers easy road cycling routes perfect for beginners or families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1243335509/astudillo-and-puente-fitero-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Astudillo and Puente Fitero Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 19.6-mile (31.5 km) route with minimal elevation gain, providing a relaxed ride through the local countryside. The region's lightly trafficked roads also contribute to a pleasant experience for all skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes near Astudillo?
A: Road cycling around Astudillo offers a diverse landscape. You can expect routes that transition from the flat expanses of the "Tierra de Campos" to areas with more rolling hills. For those seeking greater challenges, the "Montaña Palentina" further north provides more demanding ascents and spectacular natural surroundings.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Astudillo?
A: Yes, several circular routes are available. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1159593584/torquemada-from-quintana-del-puente-circular-by-the-castles" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torquemada from Quintana del Puente – circular by the Castles&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 32.1-mile (51.7 km) path that provides scenic views and passes by historical castle sites. Another great choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1170729077/torquemada-from-astudillo-circular-by-castillo-de-la-mota-and-valdeolmillos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torquemada from Astudillo – circular by Castillo de la Mota and Valdeolmillos&lt;/a&gt;, a 34.0-mile (54.8 km) trail through rolling plains and historic villages.

Q: What are some of the longer or more challenging road cycling routes in the area?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a longer and more challenging ride, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1159537186/palencia-from-quintana-del-puente-circular-via-castillo-de-sarmiento-and-torquemada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palencia from Quintana del Puente – circular via Castillo de Sarmiento and Torquemada&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 94.5-mile (152.1 km) route with significant elevation gain. This route allows you to explore more of the Palencia province's varied terrain.

Q: What can I see along the road cycling routes around Astudillo?
A: Many routes offer a blend of natural beauty and historical sites. You can encounter the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/148691" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal de Castilla&lt;/a&gt;, an 18th-century hydraulic marvel, and its impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1012824" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Quadruple Lock&lt;/a&gt;. Other points of interest include the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/849948" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Nicolás Hermitage at Puente Fitero&lt;/a&gt; and the charming settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4541372" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Frómista&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: When is the best time of year for road biking in Astudillo?
A: The Palencia province generally offers good cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are often ideal, with milder temperatures and pleasant scenery. While winter cycling is possible, it's advisable to check local weather forecasts, especially if planning to venture into the more mountainous areas of the Montaña Palentina.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints or hidden gems along the routes?
A: The region is characterized by rural charm and little-traveled roads, offering many scenic sections. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/854237" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Scenic Section of the Canal de Castilla&lt;/a&gt; is a highlight for its picturesque views. Exploring the routes often reveals charming villages and historical sites away from heavy traffic, providing a serene cycling experience.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Astudillo?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.25 stars from over 3 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, from open plains to rolling hills, and the network of well-maintained, lightly trafficked roads. The blend of natural landscapes with historic villages and cultural routes like the Palencian Romanesque also contributes to a highly enjoyable experience.

Q: Is there parking available near the road cycling routes in Astudillo?
A: Astudillo, as a historic town, serves as a convenient base for exploring the region. While specific parking areas for cyclists aren't detailed, you can typically find parking within or on the outskirts of the town, allowing easy access to the starting points of many routes.

Q: Can I find cafes or places to rest along the road cycling routes?
A: The routes often pass through charming villages and settlements in the Palencia province. While specific cafes are not listed, these villages typically offer opportunities for refreshments and rest stops, allowing you to refuel and experience the local culture during your ride.
tips: An important town on the trade route between Burgos and Palencia and Valladolid, Torquemada sits at a crossing of the Pisuerga River. The view of the church from the bridge is inspiring.
Surrounded by a landscape of cereal fields is the small town of Valdeolmillos. The view of the bell tower of the Church of San Juan Bautista will guide your way as it towers over the city.
Beautiful little castle surrounded by underground mountain dwellings
